Shopping Scams

Puppy Sale Scam Checklist: Warning Signs Before You Pay a Deposit

Emotional urgency and limited availability make pet scams especially effective.

Published 2026-03-29 · Beat the Scam Editorial Team · 1 min read

puppy scamfake breeder scampuppy deposit fraud
Key rule: verify through an official route you opened yourself, not the link, number, app, or payment details supplied by the suspicious message.
On this page
  1. Why puppy scams work
  2. Check whether the breeder is verifiable
  3. Shipping and sudden fees are common
  4. Video, local verification, and paperwork matter
  5. Avoid impulsive deposits
  6. Best practice

Why puppy scams work

People searching for a specific breed or age are often willing to move quickly. Fraudsters exploit that emotional pull with attractive photos and a story that makes delay feel risky.

Check whether the breeder is verifiable

A real breeder should have a consistent identity, traceable contact details, and evidence of actual litters rather than only polished advert copy. Reverse-image search is especially useful here.

Shipping and sudden fees are common

Scammers may accept a deposit and then invent transport, vaccination, crate, or insurance charges. Each payment is positioned as the final step. It rarely is.

Video, local verification, and paperwork matter

Ask for live video, not only pre-recorded clips. Verify licence or breeder information where relevant. A refusal to allow basic checks is a serious warning sign.

Avoid impulsive deposits

A pressure story about another buyer waiting or a same-day decision window is a manipulation tactic. Slow the process down and verify first.

Best practice

Whenever possible, view in person or use a route with strong buyer protection. Treat remote-only purchase pressure as a major risk factor.

Frequently asked questions

Can scam adverts use real puppy photos?

Yes. Images are often stolen from legitimate breeders or old adverts.

Is a deposit always unsafe?

Not always, but it increases risk if you have not independently verified the seller.

Should I trust social media breeder pages?

Only after separate verification. Social presence is easy to fake.

Think you’ve spotted a scam? Use the AI scam checker for an instant analysis, or report it to Action Fraud.

Fast checks

  • Pause before sending money or credentials
  • Verify with an official site, app, or number
  • Never share one-time passcodes
  • Be sceptical of bank transfer pressure
